campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Finance department staff presented the proposed changes to these fees, and council members debated the proposal before making a decision.
keyTopics: Annual business license tax rate increases
Changes to Campbell Municipal Code fees
Finance department budget presentation
City council public deliberation and vote

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a new rule the city is considering. After listening to public comments, the council moved forward with introducing the proposed ordinance and skipped the standard first reading step to speed up the process. The council directed staff to prepare the ordinance for a final vote at the next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input on new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite adoption
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next council meeting
Staff directed to place ordinance on consent calendar

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in April and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services to move forward with town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and financial records
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other service providers
Moving forward with town improvement projects
Three-year professional services agreement approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 22, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions. The council reviewed and finalized the agenda for their April 28 meeting, then looked ahead to plan the May 5 meeting, deciding which items to add and whether any topics needed special time slots.
keyTopics: Finalized April 28, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned items for May 5, 2026 draft agenda
Set time limits for specific agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 9-16, 2026
Addressed notice and waiver requirements for documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what money will be set aside for big projects and improvements around town. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Budget and funding for city construction projects and infrastructure improvements for 2026-27
Teen Month proclamation recognizing young people in Mountain View
National Volunteer Week proclamation honoring community volunteers

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a closed-door meeting at 6 PM to discuss confidential matters (the specific topics weren't disclosed to the public), followed by a regular public meeting at 7 PM that included a flag salute and recognition of local winners or honorees.
keyTopics: Closed session discussion (details not publicly available)
Recognition or awards ceremony for local winners
Regular council meeting convened after closed session

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 21, 2026 to discuss two pending lawsuits that could potentially cost the city money, and to recognize the work of the Administrative Services Department's Budget, Finance, and Purchasing teams. The meeting also included time for residents to speak about topics not on the formal agenda.
keyTopics: Two active lawsuits with significant financial risk to the city
Recognition of city budget, finance, and purchasing staff
Public comment period for resident concerns

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also worked on finalizing a new employment agreement with firefighters for 2026-2029, though one item was withdrawn before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New three-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (Local 1171)
Staff withdrew one agenda item before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to discuss several important financial and development matters. They held public hearings on tax responsibility rules, the city's budget and development fees for the upcoming fiscal year, and discussed plans to address homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
County homelessness prevention initiative
Updated user fees for city services
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to take another look at a decision that was already made to allow tree removal at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to reconsider whether protected trees on this property should be allowed to be cut down.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request for Planning Director to review an already-approved decision
Trees on private property and city protection rules
